info.title: Axon REST API
info.description: Dedicated REST routes for Axon discovery, RAG, crawl, ingest, and watch workflows.

200.description: SQLite, Qdrant, and TEI dependencies are ready

503.description: One or more dependencies are not ready

get.summary: Serve an artifact file from the configured output directory.
get.description:
The `path` query parameter is validated structurally and via canonicalization
before serving the file. Returns:
- `400` for structurally unsafe paths (absolute, `..`, etc.)
- `403` when the resolved path escapes the output root or is a symlink
- `404` when the file does not exist or is not a regular file

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Slash-preserving relative path within the output directory, e.g. `jobs/abc/output.md`

502.description: Upstream vector or LLM service unavailable

200.description: RAG answer streamed as server-sent events
Extracted brand identity from a URL
Direct LLM chat answer streamed as server-sent events
Markdown, metadata, and link diff between two URLs

502.description: Upstream fetch or verification service unavailable
Upstream LLM or vector service unavailable

200.description: Unified job events streamed as server-sent events

502.description: Upstream vector or embedding service unavailable

200.description: Purge result (counts of points/URLs matched or deleted)
Research synthesis streamed as server-sent events
Search results and queued crawl jobs

403.description: Source not authorized for caller scopes

429.description: Upstream LLM or search provider rate limited

502.description: Upstream search or LLM service unavailable

200.description: Brief LLM summary of scraped URL content

502.description: Upstream crawl, render, or LLM service unavailable

200.description: Brief LLM summary streamed as server-sent events

ApiError.description:
The shared, transport-neutral error shape.

Every crate reports failures as an `ApiError` so CLI, REST, MCP, jobs, logs,
and progress streams render one consistent structure.

details.description: Redacted structured context (safe key/value pairs only).

visibility.description: Where this error may be surfaced.

text_diff.description: Unified diff of the markdown content, if any changes.

mcp_candidates.description: Synthesized MCP candidate probe attempts (omitted when empty).

ErrorSeverity.description: How severe an error is and whether it terminates the item/job.

ErrorStage.description: Pipeline/transport stage an error is attributed to.

ErrorVisibility.description:
Where a context entry (or error) may be surfaced.

From the "visibility" field in the error shape: `public`, `internal`,
`sensitive`.

value.description:
**Display-formatted, not machine-readable.** Pre-rendered for direct
rendering by clients — integers carry thousands separators (`"1,024"`)
and string fields (e.g. ingest `phase`) pass through verbatim. Do not
parse this back into a number; if a surface needs the raw value, add a
typed field rather than reverse-engineering the formatting here.

JobProgress.description: Derived, transport-neutral progress for a generic async job.

percent.description: 0–100 when determinate; `None` = indeterminate (render a pulsing bar).

JobStatusResponse.description:
Typed job-status envelope so the `{ job, progress }` wire shape is a
registered OpenAPI schema (and thus reflected into the generated palette/
android clients) instead of an opaque `serde_json::Value`.

job.description:
Raw job record in the wire-compat shape (`status`, `result_json`,
timestamps, …). Still `Value` because the per-family job payloads are
heterogeneous; `progress` is the typed, cross-family projection of it.

oneOf.description:
Server-derived, transport-neutral progress for the generic async
families (embed/extract/ingest). `None` for crawl, which carries a
richer client-side snapshot rather than the generic shape.

McpCandidateAttempt.description:
One synthesized MCP candidate and its probe outcome.

`rpc_probe` is `Some` iff `outcome == Confirmed`; every other outcome
(`Unconfirmed`, `Blocked`) carries `None`.

McpHostKind.description: Which host a synthesized MCP candidate targets.

McpProbeOutcome.description: Outcome of probing one synthesized MCP candidate.

dry_run.description:
Preview only — count matches without deleting. **Defaults to `true`** for
agent safety: a bare `purge` previews; set `dry_run=false` to delete.

prefix.description: Match `target` as a prefix over a whole docs subtree / origin.

target.description:
URL (or seed-URL/origin when `prefix` is set) to delete from the index.
**Handler-required despite the `Option`:** the type is `Option<String>`
only so a missing field deserializes to a clean "target is required"
error instead of a serde rejection; `handle_purge` returns an error when
it is `None`. It is not an optional argument.

PurgeResult.description:
Result of a purge: counts of points/URLs matched (and deleted, unless this
was a `dry_run` preview).

dry_run.description: When true, nothing was deleted — counts reflect what *would* be removed.

RpcProbeResult.description:
Result of probing a discovered endpoint for JSON-RPC 2.0 / OpenRPC / MCP support.

Fields are populated according to the detected `protocol` and are otherwise
left empty: `server_name`/`server_version`/`tools` are MCP-only, `methods` is
JSON-RPC/OpenRPC-only. The flat shape preserves the wire contract; the type
does not statically prevent contradictory combinations.

oneOf.description: Detected protocol, or `null` when no protocol matched.
Transport the protocol was observed over.

RpcProtocol.description:
Detected RPC protocol family. Wire strings match the historical
stringly-typed values (`"jsonrpc2"`, `"openrpc"`, `"mcp"`).

RpcTransport.description: Transport layer the protocol was observed over.

supported_actions.description:
Legacy internal action names retained for the panel command path.

Public HTTP clients should use `supported_routes`; `/v1/actions` is no
longer mounted after the direct REST cutover.

tags.name: discovery
tags.description: Read-only source, domain, stats, status, and health endpoints

tags.name: rag
tags.description: Query, retrieve, ask, evaluate, and suggest endpoints
